snapshot.summary: Webhooks are custom callbacks the Housecall provides to developers to make them aware of useful events triggered by our Pros.
snapshot.data:
# Webhooks Webhooks are custom callbacks the Housecall provides to developers to make them aware of useful events triggered by our Pros. To have webhooks configured for your Company or Application send an email to . ## Partner Job Webhook Events Event Name | Event Key | Description ---------|----------|---------- Partner Job Accepted | partner_job.accepted | HCP accepted the partner job (should always accept available job) Partner Job Declined | partner_job.declined | HCP declined the job because of one of the following reasons:
• HCP didn’t find any organization to match the partner job requirements
• All matching organizations declined the partner job
• Partner job expired Partner Job Assigned | partner_job.assigned | HCP assigned a Pro to do the partner job Partner Job Scheduled | partner_job.scheduled | The assigned Pro set a schedule for the job Partner Job Pro En Route | partner_job.pro_en_route | The assigned Pro is on his way to the customer Partner Job Started | partner_job.started | The assigned Pro started the partner job Partner Job Completed | partner_job.completed | The assigned Pro completed the partner job Partner Job Unassigned | partner_job.unassigned | The assigned Pro canceled the job, which makes the job available to other Pros ### Partner Job Webhook Protocol #### Delivery headers Header | Description ---------|---------- Content-Type | Always equal to: 'application/json' Api-Signature | HMAC hex digest of the timestamp and request body joined by '.' respectively, generated using the SHA-256 hash function and the partners secret as the HMAC key Api-Timestamp | Timestamp of the instant the event fired #### Webhook paylooad ```json { "event": "partner_job.accepted", "partner_job": { "id": "string", "name": "string", "job_inbox_partner_id": "string", "external_id": "string", "total_amount": 75.0, "paid_amount": 0.0, "paid": "string", "expires_at": "string", "decline_reason": "string or null", "status": "open", "status_timestamps": { "accepted_at": "2021-01-01T10:00:00Z", "declined_at": "2021-01-01T10:00:00Z", "assigned_at": "2021-01-01T10:00:00Z", "scheduled_at": "2021-01-01T10:00:00Z", "pro_en_route": "2021-01-01T10:00:00Z", "started_at": "2021-01-01T10:00:00Z", "completed_at": "2021-01-01T10:00:00Z" }, "customer": { "first_name": "string", "last_name": "string", "email": "string", "phone_number": "string", "notifications_enabled": true }, "address": { "street": "string", "street_line2": "string", "city": "string", "state": "string", "zip": "string", "country": "string" }, "schedule": { "scheduled_start": "string", "scheduled_end": "string" }, "line_items": [ { "name": "string", "description": "string", "unit_price": 0.0, "quantity": 1.0 } ], "assigned_company": { // null if unassigned "id": "string", "name": "string", "phone_number": "string", "email": "string", "website": "string", "logo_url": "string", "address": { "street": "string", "street_line_2": "string", "city": "string", "state": "string", "zip": "string", "country": "string" } } } } ``` Please note that once the job gets past the `assigned` state, the payload will start including data related to the `Job` object that was created on the Service Pro's account. When the Service Pros complete the job and have added attachments, you will be able to fetch them from the provided URLs, like the following payload, but please note that these URLs expire, so if you need them, please fetch the attachments and don't just store the URLs. ```json { "event": "partner_job.completed", "partner_job": { "id": "string", "name": "string", "job_inbox_partner_id": "string", "external_id": "string", "total_amount": 75.0, "paid_amount": 0.0, "paid": "string", "expires_at": "string", "decline_reason": null, "status": "string", "can_be_there_by": "string", "status_timestamps": { "accepted_at": "string", "assigned_at": "string", "declined_at": null, "scheduled_at": "string", "pro_en_route_at": "string", "started_at": "string", "completed_at": "string" }, "partner": { "uuid": "string", "name": "string", "logo_url": "string", "email": "string", "phone": "string" }, "customer": { "first_name": "string", "last_name": "string", "email": "string", "phone_number": null, "notifications_enabled": false }, "address": { "street": "string", "street_line_2": "string", "city": "string", "state": "string", "zip": "string", "country": "string" }, "schedule": { "scheduled_start": "string", "scheduled_end": "string", "arrival_windows": [ { "start_date": "string", "end_date": "string" } ] }, "line_items": [], "assigned_company": { "id": "string", "name": "string", "phone_number": "string", "email": "string", "website": "string", "logo_url": "string", "address": { "street": "string", "street_line_2": "string", "city": "string", "state": "string", "zip": "string", "country": "string" } }, "service_category": { "id": 152, "uuid": "string", "asset_id": "string", "name": "string" }, "job": { "id": 1, "uuid": "string", "name": "string", "description": "string", "amount": 0, "organization_id": 8280, "service_pro_id": 9686, "presentable_job_status": "string", "presentable_payment_status": "string", "service_pro_name": "string", "message_from_pro": null, "display_invoice_number": "string", "note": "string", "time_zone": "string", "customer_name": "string", "customer_id": 215834, "contact_first_name": null, "contact_last_name": null, "contact_phone": null, "address": { "id": null, "street": "string", "street_line_2": "string", "city": "string", "zip": "string", "state": "string", "time_zone": "string", "country": null }, "schedule": { "start": "string", "end": "string", "arrival_window": 240 }, "status_timestamps": { "scheduled_at": "string", "on_my_way_at": "string", "started_at": "string", "completed_at": "string" }, "line_items": [], "service_pros": [ { "id": 9686, "uuid": "string", "first_name": "string", "last_name": "string", "mobile_number": "string", "email": "string", "organization_uuid": "string", "time_zone": "string", "color_hex": "string", "organization_affiliation": { "role": "string", "is_admin": true, "is_poc": false, "can_chat_with_customers": true, "can_be_booked_online": true }, "archived": false, "hcp_assist": false, "avatar_url": "string", "avatar_url_thumb_round": "string" } ], "entry_point": "string", "customer_uuid": "string", "original_estimate_id": null, "attachments": [ { "id": "string", "file_name": "string", "file_type": "string", "url": "string", "thumbnail_url": "string" } ] }, "object": "partner_job" } } ``` ## Partner Lead Webhook Events Event Name | Event Key | Description ---------|----------|---------- Partner Lead Assigned | partner_lead.assigned | The partner lead was successfully delivered to the Assigned Pro's Job Inbox Partner Lead Converted | partner_lead.converted | The assigned Pro copied the partner lead to an estimate or a job Partner Lead Scheduled | partner_lead.scheduled | The assigned Pro set a schedule for the partner lead Partner Lead Pro En Route | partner_lead.pro_en_route | The assigned Pro is on their way to the customer Partner Lead Started | partner_lead.started | The assigned Pro started the partner lead Partner Lead Completed | partner_lead.completed | The assigned Pro completed the partner lead Partner Lead Unassigned | partner_lead.unassigned | The estimate or job associated with the partner lead was deleted or cancelled Partner Lead Sent | partner_lead.sent | The assigned Pro sent the partner lead estimate ### Partner Lead Webhook Protocol #### Delivery headers Header | Description ---------|---------- Content-Type | Always equal to: 'application/json' Api-Signature | HMAC hex digest of the timestamp and request body joined by '.' respectively, generated using the SHA-256 hash function and the partners secret as the HMAC key Api-Timestamp | Timestamp of the instant the event fired #### Webhook paylooad ```json { "event": "partner_lead.completed", "partner_lead": { "id": "string", "number": 1, "job_inbox_partner_id": "string", "external_id": "string", "total_amount": 75.0, "paid_amount": 0.0, "paid": "string", "status": "string", "status_timestamps": { "scheduled_at": "string or null", "pro_en_route_at": "2021-01-01T10:00:00Z", "started_at": "2021-01-01T10:00:00Z", "completed_at": "2021-01-01T10:00:00Z" }, "partner": { "uuid": "string", "name": "string", "logo_url": "string", "email": "string", "phone": "string" }, "customer": { "first_name": "string", "last_name": "string", "email": "string", "phone_number": null, "notifications_enabled": false }, "address": { "street": "string", "street_line_2": "string", "city": "string", "state": "string", "zip": "string", "country": "string" }, "schedule": { "scheduled_start": "string", "scheduled_end": "string", "arrival_windows": [ { "start_date": "string", "end_date": "string" } ] }, "line_items": [ { "name": "string", "description": "string", "unit_price": 50.0, "quantity": 1.5 } ], "assigned_company": { // null if unassigned "id": "string", "name": "string", "phone_number": "string", "email": "string", "website": "string", "logo_url": "string", "address": { "street": "string", "street_line_2": "string", "city": "string", "state": "string", "zip": "string", "country": "string" } }, "service_category": { "id": 100, "uuid": "string", "asset_id": "string", "name": "string" }, "lead": { "id": 1, "uuid": "string", "name": "string or null", "description": "string", "amount": 7500, "organization_id": 1, "service_pro_id": 2, "presentable_job_status": "string", "presentable_payment_status": "string", "service_pro_name": "string", "message_from_pro": "string or null", "display_invoice_number": "string", "note": "string", "time_zone": "string", "customer_name": "string", "customer_id": 10, "contact_first_name": "string or null", "contact_last_name": "string or null", "contact_phone": "string or null", "address": { "id": 10, "street": "string", "street_line_2": "string", "city": "string", "zip": "string", "state": "string", "time_zone": "string", "country": null }, "schedule": { "start": "string", "end": "string", "arrival_window": 240 }, "status_timestamps": { "scheduled_at": "string", "on_my_way_at": "string", "started_at": "string", "completed_at": "string" }, "line_items": [ { "name": "string", "description": "string", "unit_price": 5000, "quantity": 1.5 } ], "service_pros": [ { "id": 9686, "uuid": "string", "first_name": "string", "last_name": "string", "mobile_number": "string", "email": "string", "organization_uuid": "string", "time_zone": "string", "color_hex": "string", "organization_affiliation": { "role": "string", "is_admin": true, "is_poc": false, "can_chat_with_customers": true, "can_be_booked_online": true }, "archived": false, "hcp_assist": false, "avatar_url": "string", "avatar_url_thumb_round": "string" } ], "entry_point": "string", "customer_uuid": "string", "original_estimate_id": null, "attachments": [ { "id": "string", "file_name": "string", "file_type": "string", "url": "string", "thumbnail_url": "string" } ] }, "object": snapshot.summary: Housecall Pro uses both API keys and OAuth 2.0 as means of authenticating with our API.
snapshot.data:
# Authentication

Housecall Pro uses both API keys and OAuth 2.0 as means of authenticating with our API. 

If you intend to use Housecall Pro’s **matching algorithm** to find Pros to fulfill your service requests, you will use an **API key** for authentication. 
- Example: Your network does not have adequate coverage to fulfill a job, and you would like Housecall Pro to source a service provider for you


If you share **mutual Pros** with Housecall Pro, and intend to send jobs or leads directly to your in-network Pros, you will use **OAuth 2.0** for authentication. 
- Example: A Pro pays you for leads, which you would like to show up on a Pro’s Housecall calendar

Housecall Pro expects the OAuth 2.0 acesss token or and API key to be included in all authenticated API requests to the server in the Authorization header that looks like the following:

### API Key

```bash
Authorization: Token 0046421dc0ba47ec87bef0c089415380
```

### OAuth 2.0

```bash
Authorization: Bearer d7f65570a4714ae944fbe58acc05ffb20c29534170ac483a3683b91f8dfe9d43
```


> It is important to note that the headers must follow these exact formats, including “Token” or “Bearer” at the beginning of your header. If you receive an authentication error, please send your cURL command to jobinbox@housecallpro.com so we may investigate.


## Setting up API Key Authorization

If you are already in the process of setting up a Partner Jobs integration with us, ask your engineering contact at Housecall Pro for an `API_KEY`.

We will create an account for you and use a third party tool to securely share your `API_KEY`. Housecall Pro expects the `API_KEY` to be included in all authenticated API requests to the server in the Authorization header.

## Setting up OAuth 2.0 Authorization

If you are already in the process of setting up a Partner Jobs integration with us, please send your callback_url to your engineering contact at Housecall Pro and we will provide you with your CLIENT_ID and CLIENT_SECRET.

Once you have both of these credentials, you may begin the `AUTHORIZATION_CODE` OAuth flow.

- Use the OAuth `AUTHORIZATION_CODE` link in order to initiate the OAuth flow.

### Authorization code link

```bash
curl -X GET https://pro.housecallpro.com/oauth/authorize?response_type=code&client_id=CLIENT_ID&redirect_uri=CALLBACK_URL
```

- After clicking the Authorization code link, the user will be prompted to log in to Housecall Pro in order to authenticate their identity, unless they are already logged in. Once authenticated, the user will be prompted to authorize the application to access their Housecall account.
- Once the user authorizes the application, the server will redirect the user's agent to the provided `CALLBACK_URL` with an `AUTHORIZATION_CODE`. (EX: https://YOUR_WEBSITE_URL/callback?code=AUTHORIZATION_CODE)
- The OAuth client will then request an access token from the api by passing the AUTHORIZATION_CODE along with the CLIENT_SECRET.

### Request access token

```bash
curl -X POST \
https://api.housecallpro.com/oauth/token \
-H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
-d '{
  "client_id": "CLIENT_ID",
  "client_secret": "CLIENT_SECRET",
  "grant_type": "authorization_code",
  "code": "AUTHORIZATION_CODE",
  "redirect_uri": "CALLBACK_URL"
}'
```

- If the authorization is successful, the api will respond with an OAuth 2.0 access token & an OAuth 2.0 refresh token to the application. You are now authorized! You may now use the token to access the pro's account through the api provided by housecall until the token expires or access is revoked.
- Our OAuth 2.0 access token have finite lifetimes, use the refresh token granted in the original token grant to request a new access token when access token is expired. Refresh tokens may be requested without user interaction by the same process as requesting an access token, changing the grant_type from `authorization_code` to `refresh_token`.

### Refresh access token

```bash
curl -X POST \
https://api.housecallpro.com/oauth/token \
-H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
-d '{
  "client_id": "CLIENT_ID",
  "client_secret": "CLIENT_SECRET",
  "grant_type": "refresh_token",
  "refresh_token": "the_refresh_token_value_goes_here",
  "redirect_uri": "CALLBACK_URL"
}'
```

openapi: 3.0.0
info:
  title: Job Inbox V1
  version: '1.0'
  contact:
    name: Housecall Engineering
    email: apideveloper@housecallpro.com
  description: HousecallPro Job Inbox API V1
servers:
  - url: 'https://api.housecallpro.com'
paths:
  /partner_jobs:
    post:
      summary: Create Partner Job
      operationId: post-partner-jobs-create
      responses:
        '200':
          $ref: '#/components/responses/PartnerJobResponse'
      description: Create a partner job
      requestBody:
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              $ref: '#/components/schemas/PartnerJobCreate'
        description: Either `customer` model or `customer_id` is required to create a partner job
      tags:
        - Partner Jobs
      parameters:
        - schema:
            type: string
          in: header
          name: 'Authorization: Token token=API_KEY'
          description: Partner API_KEY used for this value
    parameters: []
  '/partner_jobs/{partner_job_id}':
    put:
      summary: Update Partner Job
      operationId: put-partner-jobs-update
      responses:
        '200':
          $ref: '#/components/responses/PartnerJobResponse'
      description: Updates an existing partner job
      tags:
        - Partner Jobs
      requestBody:
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              $ref: '#/components/schemas/PartnerJobCreate'
      parameters:
        - schema:
            type: string
          in: header
          name: 'Authorization: Token token=API_KEY'
          description: Partner API_KEY used for this value
          required: true
    parameters:
      - schema:
          type: string
        name: partner_job_id
        in: path
        required: true
        description: The id of the partner job to update
  /partner_leads:
    post:
      summary: Create Partner Lead
      operationId: post-partner-leads-create
      responses:
        '200':
          $ref: '#/components/responses/PartnerLeadResponse'
      description: Create a partner lead
      requestBody:
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              $ref: '#/components/schemas/PartnerLeadCreate'
        description: Either `customer` model or `customer.id` is required to create a partner lead
      tags:
        - Partner Leads
      parameters:
        - schema:
            type: string
          in: header
          name: 'Authorization: Bearer ACCESS_TOKEN'
          description: OAuth ACCESS_TOKEN used for this value
    parameters: []
  '/partner_leads/{partner_lead_id}':
    put:
      summary: Update Partner Lead
      operationId: put-partner-leads-update
      responses:
        '200':
          $ref: '#/components/responses/PartnerLeadResponse'
      description: Updates an existing partner lead
      tags:
        - Partner Leads
      requestBody:
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              $ref: '#/components/schemas/PartnerLeadCreate'
      parameters:
        - schema:
            type: string
          in: header
          name: 'Authorization: Bearer ACCESS_TOKEN'
          description: OAuth ACCESS_TOKEN used for this value
          required: true
    parameters:
      - schema:
          type: string
        name: partner_lead_id
        in: path
        required: true
components:
  schemas:
    PartnerJob:
      title: PartnerJob
      x-examples: {}
      allOf:
        - type: object
          properties:
            id:
              type: string
        - $ref: '#/components/schemas/PartnerJobCreate'
    PartnerJobCreate:
      title: PartnerJobCreate
      type: object
      description: ''
      properties:
        external_id:
          type: string
          description: Your identifier for this job outside of Housecall Pro.
        service_category_name:
          type: string
          description: |
            The industry of the job, below are some examples, more values can be found in [Appendix](docs/Appendix.md) file:
             * `Carpet Cleaning`
             * `Heating & Air Conditioning`
             * `Plumbing`
             * `Electrical`
             * `Handyman`
             * `Appliances`
             * `Pest Control`
             * `Garage`
             * `Water Heater`
        service_call_amount:
          type: number
          description: Minimum trip charge amount the Pro will get paid
        estimated_amount:
          type: number
          description: Amount pro could get based on region and industry standards if he does the job
        name:
          type: string
          description: The name of this job
        customer:
          $ref: '#/components/schemas/PartnerJobCustomerCreate'
        expires_at:
          type: string
          description: Indicates the time when this job will no longer be available for distribution. After this time, the job will be auto-declined.
        schedule:
          $ref: '#/components/schemas/PartnerJobCreateSchedule'
        pricing:
          $ref: '#/components/schemas/PartnerJobCreatePricing'
        attachment_urls:
          type: array
          description: 'URLs of images, videos or files to be attached to the lead'
          items:
            type: string
        notes:
          type: string
      required:
        - external_id
        - service_category_name
        - name
        - customer
    PartnerJobCreatePricing:
      title: PartnerJobCreatePricing
      type: object
      description: Partner job pricing structure. If present, at least one field must be present.
      properties:
        cost:
          type: array
          items:
            $ref: '#/components/schemas/PartnerJobCreatePricingCost'
        amount:
          $ref: '#/components/schemas/PartnerJobCreatePricingAmount'
        payer:
          type: string
        pays_in:
          $ref: '#/components/schemas/PartnerJobCreatePaymentProcessingTime'
    PartnerJobCreatePricingAmount:
      title: PartnerJobCreatePricingAmount
      type: object
      description: If not null, at least 1 field must be present
      properties:
        estimated_amount:
          type: array
          items:
            $ref: '#/components/schemas/PartnerJobCreatePricingEstimatedAmount'
        minimum:
          $ref: '#/components/schemas/PartnerJobCreatePricingLimitedAmount'
        maximum:
          $ref: '#/components/schemas/PartnerJobCreatePricingLimitedAmount'
    PartnerJobCreatePricingEstimatedAmount:
      title: PartnerJobCreatePricingEstimatedAmount
      type: object
      description: Partner job estimated amount
      properties:
        unit:
          type: string
          nullable: true
          description: If null, a fixed amount is assumed
        currency:
          type: string
          default: 'USD'
        value:
          type: number
        max_value:
          type: number
        min_value:
          type: number
          description: If max_value is present min_value is required
      required:
        - currency
        - value
    PartnerJobCreatePricingLimitedAmount:
      title: PartnerJobCreatePricingLimitedAmount
      type: object
      description: Partner job minimum or maximum amount
      properties:
        unit:
          type: string
          nullable: true
          description: If null, a fixed amount is assumed
        currency:
          type: string
          default: 'USD'
        value:
          type: number
        allows_over:
          type: string
          enum:
            - no
            - yes
            - upon_approval
      required:
        - currency
        - value
    PartnerJobCreatePricingCost:
      title: PartnerJobCreatePricingCost
      type: object
      description: Partner job pricing cost
      properties:
        unit:
          type: string
          nullable: true
          description: If null, a fixed amount is assumed
        currency:
          type: string
          default: 'USD'
        value:
          type: number
        prepaid:
          type: boolean
          default: false
          description: 'Specifies whether this job cost was already paid by pro'
      required:
        - currency
        - value
        - prepaid
    PartnerJobCreatePaymentProcessingTime:
      title: PartnerJobCreatePaymentProcessingTime
      type: object
      description: When partner will pay pro. If you don't need this, send null on this object.
      properties:
        unit:
          type: string
          nullable: true
          enum:
            - hour
            - day
            - week
            - month
        value:
          type: number
          description: 'Number of `unit`s. Ex: 15 days'
      required:
        - unit
        - value
    PartnerJobCustomerCreate:
      title: PartnerJobCustomerCreate
      type: object
      description: 'If customer fields are not supplied, then `id` is required'
      properties:
        first_name:
          type: string
        last_name:
          type: string
        email:
          type: string
        notifications_enabled:
          type: boolean
          description: Notify customer when their jobs are updated. We recommend it for the best experience
          enum:
            - true
            - false
        phone:
          type: string
          description: 10 digits
        address:
          $ref: '#/components/schemas/PartnerJobAddressCreate'
        id:
          type: string
          description: ID of a known customer. Required if other customer details are not entered
      required:
        - first_name
        - last_name
        - notifications_enabled
    PartnerJobAddressCreate:
      title: PartnerJobAddressCreate
      type: object
      properties:
        street:
          type: string
        street_line_2:
          type: string
        city:
          type: string
        state:
          type: string
        zip:
          type: string
        country:
          type: string
      required:
        - street
        - city
        - state
        - zip
    PartnerLead:
      title: PartnerLead
      x-examples: {}
      allOf:
        - type: object
          properties:
            id:
              type: string
        - $ref: '#/components/schemas/PartnerLeadCreate'
    PartnerLeadCreate:
      title: PartnerLeadCreate
      type: object
      description: ''
      properties:
        external_id:
          type: string
          description: Your identifier for this job outside of Housecall Pro
        service_category_name:
          type: string
          description: |
            The industry of the job, below are some examples, more values can be found in [Appendix](docs/Appendix.md) file:
             * `Carpet Cleaning`
             * `Heating & Air Conditioning`
             * `Plumbing`
             * `Electrical`
             * `Handyman`
             * `Appliances`
             * `Pest Control`
             * `Garage`
             * `Water Heater`
        line_items:
          type: array
          minItems: 1
          description: The first line item name will be the name of the lead
          items:
            $ref: '#/components/schemas/PartnerLeadLineItem'
        attachment_urls:
          type: array
          description: 'URLs of images, videos or files to be attached to the lead'
          items:
            type: string
        customer:
          $ref: '#/components/schemas/PartnerLeadCustomerCreate'
        notes:
          type: string
          description: Notes for the pro to see when receiving this lead
        schedule:
          $ref: '#/components/schemas/Schedule'
        fees:
          type: array
          items:
            $ref: '#/components/schemas/PartnerFee'
      required:
        - line_items
        - customer
    PartnerLeadCustomerCreate:
      title: PartnerLeadCustomerCreate
      type: object
      description: 'If customer fields are not supplied, then `id` is required'
      properties:
        first_name:
          type: string
        last_name:
          type: string
        email:
          type: string
        notifications_enabled:
          type: boolean
          description: Notify customer when their jobs are updated. We recommend it for the best experience
        phone:
          type: string
          description: 10 digits
        address:
          $ref: '#/components/schemas/PartnerLeadAddressCreate'
        id:
          type: string
          description: ID of a known customer. Required if other customer details are not entered
      required:
        - first_name
        - last_name
        - phone
    PartnerLeadAddressCreate:
      title: PartnerLeadAddress
      type: object
      properties:
        street:
          type: string
        street_line_2:
          type: string
        city:
          type: string
        state:
          type: string
        zip:
          type: string
        country:
          type: string
    PartnerLeadLineItem:
      title: PartnerLeadLineItem
      x-stoplight:
        id: 4gwo0c8jc0b7i
      type: object
      description: ''
      properties:
        name:
          type: string
        kind:
          type: string
          description: |-
            Kind of this line item:
            - `labor`
            - `materials`
          default: labor
        unit_price:
          type: integer
          description: |-
            Amount in cents

            e.g. Value of 500 maps to $5.00
          default: 0
        quantity:
          type: number
          default: '1,0'
      required:
        - name
    PartnerJobCreateSchedule:
      title: Schedule
      type: object
      description: Date and time for the pro to arrive at the customer site. This can be exact or flexible. Leave null if no schedule is intended.
      properties:
        time_zone:
          type: string
          description: 'Time zone of given day and time ranges (ex: `America/Los_Angeles`). See [Appendix](docs/Appendix.md) for the full list.'
        arrival_windows:
          type: array
          description: 'If left empty, job will be unscheduled when accepted.'
          items:
            $ref: '#/components/schemas/ArrivalWindow'
      required:
        - time_zone
      x-examples:
        example-1:
          time_zone: string
          arrival_windows:
            - start_date: string
              end_date: string
              time_ranges:
                - start_time: string
                  end_time: string
        'example-2: 2022/04/22':
          time_zone: America/Los_Angeles
          arrival_windows:
            - start_date: '2022-04-22'
              end_date: null
              time_ranges: null
        'example-3: 2022/04/22 between 9am-12pm or 3pm-5pm':
          time_zone: America/Los_Angeles
          arrival_windows:
            - start_date: '2022-04-22'
              end_date: null
              time_ranges:
                - start_time: '9:00'
                  end_time: '12:00'
                - start_time: '15:00'
                  end_time: '17:00'
        'example-4: 2022/04/22 or 2022/04/23 between 9am-12pm or 3pm-5pm':
          time_zone: America/Los_Angeles
          arrival_windows:
            - start_date: '2022-04-22'
              end_date: '2022-04-23'
              time_ranges:
                - start_time: '9:00'
                  end_time: '12:00'
                - start_time: '15:00'
                  end_time: '17:00'
    Schedule:
      title: Schedule
      type: object
      description: Date and time for the pro to arrive at the customer site. This can be exact or flexible.
      properties:
        time_zone:
          type: string
          description: 'Time zone of given day and time ranges (ex: `America/Los_Angeles`). See [Appendix](docs/Appendix.md) for the full list.'
        arrival_windows:
          type: array
          description: 'If left emtpy, it will be considered that any date is available'
          items:
            $ref: '#/components/schemas/ArrivalWindow'
      required:
        - time_zone
      x-examples:
        example-1:
          time_zone: string
          arrival_windows:
            - start_date: string
              end_date: string
              time_ranges:
                - start_time: string
                  end_time: string
        'example-2: 2022/04/22':
          time_zone: America/Los_Angeles
          arrival_windows:
            - start_date: '2022-04-22'
              end_date: null
              time_ranges: null
        'example-3: 2022/04/22 between 9am-12pm or 3pm-5pm':
          time_zone: America/Los_Angeles
          arrival_windows:
            - start_date: '2022-04-22'
              end_date: null
              time_ranges:
                - start_time: '9:00'
                  end_time: '12:00'
                - start_time: '15:00'
                  end_time: '17:00'
        'example-4: 2022/04/22 or 2022/04/23 between 9am-12pm or 3pm-5pm':
          time_zone: America/Los_Angeles
          arrival_windows:
            - start_date: '2022-04-22'
              end_date: '2022-04-23'
              time_ranges:
                - start_time: '9:00'
                  end_time: '12:00'
                - start_time: '15:00'
                  end_time: '17:00'
    ArrivalWindow:
      title: ArrivalWindow
      x-stoplight:
        id: uqxgogy7ifl6e
      type: object
      properties:
        start_date:
          type: string
          description: Available start date (ex. `2021-06-29`)
        end_date:
          type: string
          description: 'Available end date (ex. `2021-06-29`). If not supplied, defaults to same value as `start_date`'
        time_ranges:
          type: array
          description: 'If left emtpy, it will be considered that any time during the given date is available'
          items:
            $ref: '#/components/schemas/TimeRange'
      required:
        - start_date
    TimeRange:
      title: TimeRange
      x-stoplight:
        id: vo573b6kd3da0
      type: object
      properties:
        start_time:
          type: string
          description: |-
            Start time (ex. `16:30:00`)

            NOTE: Must be before end time
        end_time:
          type: string
          description: |-
            End time (ex. `17:30:00`)

            NOTE: Must be after start time
      required:
        - start_time
    PartnerFee:
      title: PartnerFee
      x-stoplight:
        id: yregaxwzjm5ak
      type: object
      description: ''
      properties:
        name:
          type: string
        amount:
          type: integer
          description: |-
            Amount in cents

            e.g. Value of 500 maps to $5.00
  securitySchemes:
    Company API Key:
      name: API Key
      type: apiKey
      in: header
      description: |-
        Company API Key Authentication.

        Authentication Header value format [Token {api-key}]
    Application API Key:
      name: API Key
      type: apiKey
      in: header
      description: |-
        Application API Key Authentication.

        Authentication Header value format [Token {api-key}]
    Housecall User OAuth Token:
      type: oauth2
      flows:
        clientCredentials:
          tokenUrl: ''
          refreshUrl: ''
          scopes: {}
  responses:
    PartnerJobResponse:
      description: Partner Job Response
      content:
        application/json:
          schema:
            $ref: '#/components/schemas/PartnerJob'
        application/xml:
          schema:
            type: object
            properties: {}
    PartnerLeadResponse:
      description: Example response
      content:
        application/json:
          schema:
            $ref: '#/components/schemas/PartnerLead'
  examples: {}
  parameters: {}
tags:
  - name: Partner Jobs
